findings.title: Keep runtime nested as infrastructure, not a workflow mode
findings.claim: The target system-deep-loop layout is sound only if runtime remains out of mode-registry and does not carry nested graph metadata as a workflow packet.
findings.recommendation: Preserve runtime/ as an internal infrastructure subtree under system-deep-loop and keep workflow modes limited to deep-research, deep-review, deep-ai-council, and deep-improvement.

findings.title: Add replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs to internal path-repair scope
findings.claim: A live deep-ai-council script hardcodes old deep-loop-runtime script paths and should be repaired during child 002 internal path rewrites.
findings.recommendation: Patch child 002 Stage 3a or its implementation checklist to include this script alongside other internal workflow-to-runtime path repairs.

findings.title: Runtime dependency self-containment is partial
findings.claim: Runtime package dependencies are local, but TypeScript tooling and type roots still borrow system-spec-kit paths.
findings.recommendation: Keep the structural merge scoped to path-depth repair and avoid claiming full tooling decoupling until a separate cleanup packet owns it.

findings.title: system-spec-kit council test discovery must move to nested runtime paths
findings.claim: system-spec-kit still references runtime council tests through old relative paths and must be updated after nesting.
findings.recommendation: Repair the reverse test-discovery paths in the same structural phase as runtime nesting and keep system-spec-kit council tests in the closeout gate.

findings.title: Advisor merged-skill identity is duplicated across Python and TypeScript
findings.claim: The old deep-loop-workflows identity appears in advisor path constants and merged-id aliases, so migration must update both implementations and regenerate projections.
findings.recommendation: Update Python, TypeScript, drift tests, generated projections, and corpus expectations as one advisor migration unit, followed by validation/rebaseline.

findings.title: OpenCode and Claude agent mirrors both carry old deep-loop paths
findings.claim: Mirrored agents need paired updates and mirror-sync validation after the skill identity and path migration.
findings.recommendation: Treat agent mirrors as a synchronized migration surface and run the mirror-sync gate before closeout.

findings.title: fallback-router.ts is tested but effectively unwired
findings.claim: The fallback router has typed routing and tests, but current fanout retry handling requeues the same item/model and records retry_exhausted after max retries without substitution.
findings.recommendation: Keep fallback-router wiring optional for the structural merge; if child 004 proceeds, let fanout-run own model fallback policy and integrate through a retry-exhausted seam.

findings.title: Closeout must remove compatibility shims and pass full migration gates
findings.claim: Temporary compatibility paths are only acceptable during migration; parent success requires no old top-level skill folders and clean residual old-id grep outside allowlists.
findings.recommendation: Block completion on runtime tests, system-spec-kit council tests, advisor rebaseline, divergence ratchet, mirror sync, residual grep, and deletion of temporary symlinks/shims.
